Class StringBackend<M>

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.pp.StringBackend<M>
public class StringBackend<M> extends Object
A Backend which appends all output to a StringBuffer. The mark(Object o) method does nothing in this implementation. There is a method count() which returns the number of characters written by this so far. The method Object.toString() gets the output written so far.

    • out

      protected final StringBuilder out

  • Constructor Details

    • StringBackend

      public StringBackend()
      Create a new StringBackend. This will accumulate output in a fresh, private StringBuffer.

  • Method Details

    • print

      public void print(String s)
      Append a String s to the output. s contains no newlines.

    • newLine

      public void newLine()
      Start a new line.

    • mark

      public void mark(M o)
      Gets called to record a mark() call in the input.

      public int count()
      Returns the number of characters written through this backend.

    • result

      public String result()
      Returns the accumulated output
